dependencies: Third-party service providers including cloud providers for data storage and processing
Media providers and production companies for client services
External service providers for information technology systems
risks: Cybersecurity threats and attacks on information technology systems
Security breaches and unauthorized access to data by third-party service providers
Risk of material loss from media purchases and production costs if clients default on payment
Sequential liability risk where agencies may be liable to media providers before client payment
Increased cybersecurity risks from remote work environments
Dependence on third-party cloud providers subject to malicious attacks

regulatory_risks: Government regulations limiting scope and content of marketing and communications services
Regulations limiting tax deductibility of advertising expenditures
Federal, state, and foreign data privacy laws (evolving requirements)
European Union Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (ESRS requirements)
UN Global Compact principles and UN Sustainable Development Goals compliance
Science Based Target initiative (SBTi) emissions reduction requirements
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and anti-corruption/anti-bribery laws
Global Anti-Base Erosion (Pillar Two) minimum effective tax rate of 15% for multinational companies
OECD Pillar Two model rules implementation across jurisdictions
Regulatory requirements for AI use (ethical considerations, intellectual property, privacy)
New laws and guidance limiting use of AI technology
Increased scrutiny from governmental authorities due to business size and complexity post-merger
Environmental, social and governance (ESG) reporting requirements across multiple jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions imposed on Russia by United States, United Kingdom, and European Union
Suspension of business operations in Ukraine due to war
Disposition of all businesses in Russia in 2022 due to war in Ukraine
Currency repatriation restrictions in high-growth markets and developing countries
Commercial laws in developing countries that are undeveloped, vague, inconsistently enforced, retroactively applied or frequently changed
